principle.name: Traceability is mandatory
principle.description: Every HLD section must trace to a user story; every user story must trace to a PRD requirement. No orphaned content.
principle.rationale: Traceability enables impact analysis, test coverage verification, and prevents scope creep.

principle.name: Scope boundaries must be explicit
principle.description: Each user story and HLD section must have explicit in-scope and out-of-scope. No gray areas.
principle.rationale: Vague boundaries cause overlap, gaps, and implementation confusion.

principle.name: Acceptance criteria must be testable
principle.description: Every acceptance criterion must be observable and verifiable. DENY "it should work correctly" — demand specific observable conditions.
principle.rationale: Untestable criteria cannot be validated and lead to subjective completion judgments.

principle.name: User stories are user-outcome groupings, not technical modules
principle.description: User stories group by user outcome or feature area, not by technical layer (e.g., "frontend" vs "backend").
principle.rationale: Technical-module grouping hides dependencies and creates artificial boundaries.

principle.name: HLD sections sized for one implementation cycle
principle.description: Each HLD section must be small enough for one focused implementation cycle. If too broad, split it.
principle.rationale: Oversized sections create long-running work items and block parallel progress.

principle.name: Project Scaffolding is always first
principle.description: US-001 is always "Project Scaffolding and Environment Setup" — repository, structure, tooling, CI, docs.
principle.rationale: All other work depends on a working development environment.

pitfall.description: Orphaned HLD sections with no PRD traceability
pitfall.why_problematic: Content without traceability cannot be validated against requirements and may be scope creep.
pitfall.correct_approach: Every HLD section must have parent linkage to a user story and PRD section.

pitfall.why_problematic: "It should work correctly" cannot be tested. Implementation becomes subjective.
pitfall.correct_approach: Use Given/When/Then or explicit bullet checks with observable conditions.

pitfall.description: User stories grouped by technical layer
pitfall.why_problematic: Technical grouping hides cross-cutting concerns and creates artificial dependencies.
pitfall.correct_approach: Group by user outcome or feature area from PRD section 7.

pitfall.description: HLD sections too broad for one cycle
pitfall.why_problematic: Oversized sections block parallel work and create long-running items.
pitfall.correct_approach: Split HLD sections until each is implementable in one focused cycle.

pitfall.description: Sibling overlap between user stories or HLD sections
pitfall.why_problematic: Overlap causes duplicate work, conflicting implementations, and unclear ownership.
pitfall.correct_approach: Check for overlap; require explicit boundary rewrite before proceeding.

category.name: before_user_story_decomposition
category.item: PRD section 7 user story groups are extracted.
Security requirements (if any) are noted for design impact.

category.name: before_hld_drafting
category.item: US-001-scaffolding exists as first user story.
All user stories have scope boundaries and testable acceptance criteria.
No sibling overlap in user stories.

category.name: before_completion
category.item: All HLD sections trace to user stories and PRD.
No sibling overlap in HLD sections.
Each HLD section is sized for one implementation cycle.
